reforms.description: The Austrian government plans to close the technology gap with other industrialized countries by increasing technology and research programs to 1.5% of GDP by 1990, focusing on micro-electronics and biotechnology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing investment in technology and research is expected to enhance competitiveness and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan is stated as a government initiative to be implemented by 1990.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D investment in key sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Closing the technology gap vis-à-vis other industrialised countries by stepping-up technology and research programmes involving resources (public and private) to the tune of 1.5 per cent of GDP by 1990.

reforms.description: A new company law (Gewerbeordnung) is introduced to encourage the creation of small firms and improve competitiveness, alongside better diffusion and adaptation to new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging small firm creation and technology adaptation is expected to foster innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new company law has been put into effect to support small firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new company law represents a major structural change in the regulatory environment for businesses.
reforms.source_quote: A new company law (Gewerbeordnung) should encourage the creation of small firms and improve competitiveness along with better diffusion of, and adaptation to new technologies.
